destinations.evidence: The port RPC helper now computes effective_limit as limit.unwrap_or(200).min(capacity), then passes that value to recent_scheduled_incidents. This preserves newest-first ordering, capacity clamping, retainedCount/capacity/enabled fields, and stored revertSelector/revertReason mapping.

equivalence_argument: Mapped in the port after the local parity fix: ritual_getScheduledIncidents defaults omitted limits to 200, clamps supplied limits to capacity, returns newest-first incidents, and preserves retainedCount/capacity/enabled plus revert metadata fields.

related_but_insufficient_code: Before the local parity fix, get_scheduled_incidents used recent_scheduled_incidents(limit.unwrap_or(capacity)).

notes: Mapped after local parity fix: omitted limit now defaults to 200 before capacity clamping.
rationale: The latest incident RPC candidate merges the limit/capacity behavior and revert metadata shape into one current response invariant.
statement: ritual_getScheduledIncidents must return scheduled incidents newest-first, default omitted limits to 200, clamp supplied limits to incident capacity, expose retainedCount/capacity/enabled, and include stored revertSelector and revertReason metadata.
suggested_falsification_tests: Call ritual_getScheduledIncidents without a limit after recording more than 200 incidents; expected 200, current code returns capacity-limited count.
Call with limit > capacity and assert clamp to capacity.
summary: Scheduled incident diagnostics are mapped; omitted limit defaults to 200 and supplied limits clamp to capacity.
test_or_falsification_candidate: RPC default-limit and capacity-clamp tests for ritual_getScheduledIncidents.
title: Scheduled incident RPC returns bounded newest-first diagnostics
